search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae pneumonia follow-up clinical guideline PDF UAE
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng250 pneumonia follow-up recovery reassessment chest X-ray
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Antibiotic names, selection, and doses were outside this documentation-only mapping.
Non-official protocols were not accepted as authoritative UAE sources.
population_applicability: Partial: NG250 contains adult and child recommendations with recommendation-specific qualifiers, while the legacy workflow has no age or inpatient-discharge filter.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for pneumonia recovery, reassessment, hospital biomarker context, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph decisions, not a universal follow-up template.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local investigation, referral, and follow-up pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NG250 recommendations, including the January 2026 minor update, and NG12 updated 2026-04-15 were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG250 and NG12 remain current official NICE guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, and chest-auscultation fields without asserting severity.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pneumonia follow-up terms, symptom recovery, assessed negatives, worsening or non-improving prompts, and safety documentation with source qualifiers preserved.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped reassessment, persistent signs, referral, and hospital CRP-review fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed or clinician-decided follow-up chest-radiograph fields in the qualified post-inpatient context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports the mapped haemoptysis assessed-negative prompt as a respiratory warning feature.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of unexplained haemoptysis and age- and smoking-context combinations involving unexplained cough, fatigue, breathlessness, chest pain, weight loss, appetite loss, recurrent chest infection, clubbing, lymphadenopathy, or chest signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy workflow does not preserve every age, community-treatment, hospital-treatment, or discharge qualifier.

Chest infection alias, medication adherence, appetite or hydration without child qualifier, chest percussion, generic blood tests, incomplete imaging resolution as a red flag, physiotherapy, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some follow-up fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, severity, imaging interpretation, treatment, referral urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
